prose:
Method provenance block
• Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) gap, eV  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted)  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz”); single-point energy/orbital calculation, no re-optimisation at DFT (`DFT//input-geometry`).  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, RKS).  
• Software/backend: PySCF (via chemkit), frontier-orbitals skill.  
• Convergence: SCF converged in 7 cycles; integrity check status “ok”, trustworthy = true. No warnings were reported.

Result
• HOMO energy = –8.6037183 eV  
• LUMO energy =  +0.33695037 eV  
• HOMO–LUMO gap = **8.9406687 eV**

Reproducibility
The job ran with the command recorded in the result JSON:
`/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmdt7pjq2/live.json`
A live engine log was written during execution (path echoed at launch; visible in the working directory) and contains full SCF and integral-screening details. All numeric values above are taken directly from the chemkit JSON; no additional rounding has been applied.
